During a run‑away attempt with six older girls, Yung Wing was punished by being placed in a row with a placard 'Head of the Runaways' and a foolscap on his head, while ginger snaps and oranges were distributed to other pupils; he felt humiliated as mischievous fellows took furtive glances and made wry faces.
Yung Wing authored the book 'My Life in China and America', published in November 1909 by Henry Holt and Company, dedicated to his sons Morrison Brown and Bartlett Golden Yung.
Yung Wing was admitted to Mrs. Gutzlaff's school in 1835 when he was about seven years old.

User passed Yale entrance exams with fifteen months of Latin, twelve months of Greek, and ten months of mathematics, struggled academically, but won first prize twice in English composition competitions.
User graduated in the class of 1854, becoming the first Chinaman to graduate from a first‑class American college, served as librarian for debating societies, and attracted significant attention.
